title: Restart hidden keep-alive popup on cwd mismatch
description:
## Why

Hidden keep-alive popups currently preserve the original process across toggles. That is correct for ordinary popup replacement, but it is surprising for Yazelix Next's agent popup when the focused tab/workspace cwd changes: `Alt Shift L` can reveal a hidden `codex resume` process that still belongs to the old cwd. Agents are ephemeral and resumable, so restarting on cwd mismatch is acceptable and less surprising.

## Evidence

- `src/main.rs:231`: `launch_fallback_cwd` already derives the current focused pane cwd for popup launches.
- `src/main.rs:145`: toggle focus path currently focuses any matching hidden keep-alive popup without checking whether it was launched from the current fallback cwd.
- `src/popup_contract.rs:462`: popup identity currently matches by pane title or command marker, not by launch cwd.
- `src/popup_contract.rs:1283`: existing regression coverage protects hiding displaced keep-alive popups instead of killing them; this should remain intact.

## Scope

In scope:
- Track enough launch cwd metadata, pane cwd, or comparable state to detect when a hidden keep-alive popup belongs to a different cwd than the current focused pane fallback cwd.
- On `toggle`, when a matching hidden keep-alive popup exists but cwd differs, close that old popup and open a fresh one from the current fallback cwd.
- Add focused contract tests for same-cwd reuse and cwd-mismatch restart behavior.

Out of scope:
- YZN config changes; YZN should continue using `toggle_close_behavior "hide"` for the agent popup.
- Multiple simultaneous workspace-scoped agents per popup id.
- Changing normal close behavior or `on_close` semantics for explicit `close` actions.
- Restarting visible/focused keep-alive popups just because cwd changed elsewhere.

## Implementation Notes

1. Decide the smallest reliable cwd signal available from Zellij pane state after `hide_pane_with_id` and `show_pane_with_id`.
2. Extend the toggle planning model so a hidden keep-alive candidate can yield a restart/replace plan when its cwd mismatches the current fallback cwd.
3. Keep same-cwd hidden keep-alive panes on the current `Focus` path.
4. Ensure cwd-mismatch replacement closes the stale pane before opening a new one, so the popup id does not fan out into multiple hidden agent panes.

## Verification

- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` should exit 0.
- `cargo test --lib` should exit 0 and include regression coverage for same-cwd reuse plus cwd-mismatch restart.
- `cargo build --target wasm32-wasip1 --profile release` should exit 0.
- `nix build .#yazelix_zellij_popup --no-link` should exit 0.
- After a YZN lock update, manual dogfood should confirm: launch agent in cwd A, hide/replace it, change the tab/workspace cwd to B, press `Alt Shift L`, and observe a fresh `codex resume` in cwd B.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if Zellij does not expose enough stable cwd information for hidden/suppressed panes to distinguish same-cwd reuse from cwd-mismatch replacement.
- Stop if the implementation would require YZN-specific popup behavior in this generic plugin.
- Stop if preserving same-cwd keep-alive behavior conflicts with the new cwd-mismatch behavior.

## Maintenance Notes

- Rejected alternative: make YZN add a separate restart key. That avoids plugin state but leaves the default toggle surprising.
- Rejected alternative: scope popup identity by cwd and keep multiple hidden agents. That may be useful later, but the requested minimal behavior is to replace the old ephemeral agent.
- Rust dependency decision: prefer in-house/std; no new production dependency is expected for comparing cwd strings or paths.
close_reason: Implemented should_restart_suppressed_popup + Focus-path restart on cwd mismatch; tests cover same-cwd reuse, mismatch restart, unknown cwd.

title: Reuse suppressed keep-alive popup panes
notes: Implemented suppressed-pane-aware popup reuse. TransientPaneSnapshot now carries is_suppressed from Zellij PaneInfo. Toggle/focus/close selection treats floating and suppressed panes as live candidates, preferring focused then visible panes, so hidden keep-alive btm resolves to Focus(existing_pane_id) instead of Open. Displaced configured popup cleanup now uses visible-only selection so hidden keep-alive panes are not closed as clutter. Added regression tests for suppressed btm reuse, visible-over-suppressed preference, and cleanup ignoring suppressed panes. Validation passed: cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo test --lib; cargo check --bin yzpp; cargo build --target wasm32-wasip1 --profile release; nix build .#yazelix_zellij_popup --no-link; main nix build .#runtime --override-input yazelixZellijPopup ../yazelix-zellij-popup --no-link --no-write-lock-file. Not yet pushed/consumed in main flake.lock because runtime behavior still needs manual testing before the coupled release transaction.

title: Add plugin-level default popup margins
description:
## Why

Integrations currently have to repeat `side_margin` and `vertical_margin` inside every configured popup spec when they want a consistent popup inset. Yazelix Next now has a single semantic `[popup]` margin setting that applies to every managed popup, and that shape is useful for plain `yzpp` users too. The plugin already owns popup geometry fields, so it should also own a generic default-margin mechanism with per-popup overrides.

## Evidence

- `src/popup_contract.rs:13`: `yzpp` currently defaults side margin to `0`.
- `src/popup_contract.rs:14`: `yzpp` currently defaults vertical margin to `0`.
- `src/popup_contract.rs:47`: `TransientPopupSpec` already owns `side_margin`.
- `src/popup_contract.rs:52`: `TransientPopupSpec` already owns `vertical_margin`.
- `src/popup_contract.rs:565`: configured popup width and height are parsed through plugin-owned defaults.
- `src/popup_contract.rs:567`: configured popup side margin is parsed per popup.
- `src/popup_contract.rs:568`: configured popup vertical margin is parsed per popup.
- `/home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-next/config.kdl:12`: Yazelix Next repeats `side_margin 1` inside managed popup specs.
- `/home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-next/config.kdl:13`: Yazelix Next repeats `vertical_margin 0` inside managed popup specs.

## Scope

In scope:
- Add a plugin-level default margin surface for configured popup specs.
- Preserve existing per-popup `side_margin` and `vertical_margin` fields as overrides.
- Keep current behavior when no new default margin surface is configured.
- Choose a clear KDL shape, such as `popup_defaults { side_margin 1; vertical_margin 0; }`, only after checking the existing Zellij plugin configuration map shape.
- Add focused tests proving default margins apply to named popups and per-popup margins override the defaults.
- Update README/examples only if the public hand-written config surface changes.

Out of scope:
- Do not add Yazelix-specific config names, runtime paths, or wrapper commands.
- Do not move generated popup specs, keybindings, menu/config popup policy, or close hooks into this repo.
- Do not change popup lifecycle behavior.
- Do not change width/height semantics unless the same default mechanism naturally covers them with no extra policy.
- Do not require Yazelix Next to consume this in the same commit.

## Implementation Notes

1. Inspect how Zellij passes plugin configuration to `ConfiguredPopupSpecs::from_configuration`.
2. Add a small defaults parser near the existing `popup`/`popups` parser.
3. Apply defaults while building each configured spec, before per-popup overrides.
4. Keep default validation behavior strict: invalid default margins should not silently produce surprising geometry.
5. Keep the standalone examples readable for plain Zellij users.

## Verification

- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` should exit 0.
- `cargo test --lib` should exit 0 and cover default margin behavior.
- `cargo build --target wasm32-wasip1 --profile release` should exit 0.
- `nix build .#yazelix_zellij_popup --no-link` should exit 0.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the Zellij plugin configuration API cannot express defaults without an awkward compatibility surface.
- Stop if default margins require Yazelix-specific config policy.
- Stop if adding defaults makes hand-written plain-Zellij config harder to understand than repeating per-popup fields.

## Maintenance Notes

This is the narrow child-owned extraction that survived the Yazelix Next popup-renderer ownership decision: margins are generic popup geometry, while generated specs, semantic keybindings, command selection, and runtime injection stay in the integrating repo.
notes: Dependency decision 2026-07-03: use no new production or dev crates. The implementation is small KDL-field parsing over existing `kdl` and std collections already used by `popup_contract.rs`. Build default-margin parsing in-house next to the existing popup config parser. Rejected alternatives: adding a schema/config crate would add dependency surface for a few fields; moving this to Yazelix Next would keep generic popup geometry outside the plugin owner.
close_reason: Completed: added popup_defaults default margin parsing with per-popup overrides, docs/example, and verified cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo test --lib; cargo build --target wasm32-wasip1 --profile release; nix build .#yazelix_zellij_popup --no-link.

title: Preserve wasm Rust toolchain after preBuild
description: Child implementation for main Yazelix issue #625. The package buildPhase must select the wasm-capable Fenix Cargo/Rustc/PATH before runHook preBuild, preserve that selected environment across preBuild, export CARGO_BUILD_RUSTC, and serialize the wasm cargo build so Darwin hooks cannot leave Cargo resolving a Rust toolchain without wasm32-wasip1 std/core.
acceptance_criteria: Done when the package selects Fenix CARGO/RUSTC/PATH before preBuild, restores that environment after preBuild, exports CARGO_BUILD_RUSTC, uses the wasm-capable toolchain for cargo build, serializes the wasm build, and nix build .#yazelix_zellij_popup --no-link passes.
notes: Main public issue: https://github.com/luccahuguet/yazelix/issues/625. Main release bead: yazelix-fhfdu.
close_reason: Implemented preserve-across-preBuild wasm toolchain handling. buildPhase now sets Fenix CARGO/RUSTC/PATH before runHook preBuild, saves those values, restores them after preBuild, exports CARGO_BUILD_RUSTC, checks the wasm32-wasip1 target libdir, and runs serialized cargo build. Metadata now declares wasmToolchainPreservedAcrossPreBuild. Verified nix build .#yazelix_zellij_popup --no-link --no-write-lock-file.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Reopened: Reopened after Tyce confirmed the previous post-preBuild reconstruction still failed on Darwin; fix needs to preserve the selected wasm toolchain across preBuild.

title: Use per-pane hide/show for keep-alive popups
description: Keep-alive popup toggles currently hide the whole floating layer through hide_floating_panes(None). That is too broad for monitor panes such as btm and can leave the popup feeling unresponsive after repeated hide/show toggles. Use Zellij's pane-specific suppression APIs for toggle_close_behavior=hide, while preserving close behavior for normal transient popups.
acceptance_criteria: Complete when focused keep-alive toggles suppress only the target popup pane, a later toggle shows/focuses the same pane without launching a second process, normal close behavior still closes the pane and runs on_close, README wording no longer describes keep-alive as hiding the entire floating layer, and cargo/nix package checks pass.
notes: Rust dependency gate: no new crates. Use existing zellij_tile prelude pane lifecycle APIs hide_pane_with_id/show_pane_with_id; keep all protocol parsing in-house.
close_reason: Implemented per-pane keep-alive popup lifecycle: focused hide calls hide_pane_with_id on the target pane, later focus/toggle restores with show_pane_with_id, normal close still closes the pane and runs on_close, and README wording no longer describes keep-alive as hiding the floating layer. Verified cargo fmt --all -- --check, cargo test --lib, cargo check --bin yzpp, cargo build --target wasm32-wasip1 --profile release with rustup wasm32-wasip1, nix build .#yazelix_zellij_popup --no-link, and git diff --check.

title: Add on_hide and default popup lifecycle hooks
description:
## Why

`yzpp` already supports per-popup `on_close`, but keep-alive popups intentionally use `toggle_close_behavior "hide"` so their process state survives toggles. That preserves tools such as LazyGit, agents, and monitors, but integrations have no generic lifecycle hook when the popup is hidden or displaced. Integrations also have to repeat hook blocks on every popup if they want a consistent side effect such as refreshing a managed sidebar.

The generic plugin can remove that repeated integration glue by owning hook defaults in `popup_defaults`: plain Zellij users still opt in explicitly, while integrations such as Yazelix Next can declare one default close/hide hook and keep each popup spec focused on the popup command.

## Evidence

- `README.md:89`: popup specs support `on_close`, described as a command hook for close behavior.
- `README.md:98`: `popup_defaults` currently shares popup geometry defaults across configured popups.
- `README.md:119`: only per-popup margin overrides are documented today.
- `README.md:135`: `on_close` runs only when `yzpp` closes a pane through `toggle` or `close`, not when the child exits.
- `README.md:147`: `toggle_close_behavior "hide"` hides a pane without killing the process and later shows the same pane.
- `src/main.rs:181`: focused popup toggle branches on `toggle_close_behavior`.
- `src/main.rs:190`: the hide branch calls `hide_popup` with no hook.
- `src/main.rs:324`: displacing another configured popup branches on close versus hide.
- `src/main.rs:329`: displaced keep-alive popups are hidden with no hook.
- `src/main.rs:341`: close path closes the pane and then runs `on_close`.
- `src/main.rs:349`: `hide_popup` only hides the pane and responds `hidden`.
- `src/popup_contract.rs:45`: popup specs currently store `on_close` but no hide lifecycle hook.
- `src/popup_contract.rs:655`: `parse_popup_defaults` currently parses only scalar margin defaults.
- `src/popup_contract.rs:668`: invalid unknown `popup_defaults` fields fail the config, so hook defaults need an explicit parser path.

## User-Visible Behavior

Configured popups can declare `on_hide { ... }`. When `yzpp` hides that popup because the focused popup is toggled or because another configured popup displaces it, `yzpp` runs the configured argv hook from the same fallback-cwd model as `on_close`.

`popup_defaults` can declare generic `on_close` and `on_hide` hooks inherited by configured popup specs that do not define their own hook. Existing per-popup hooks still work and override the corresponding default. Existing `on_close` remains the close-only hook. Showing, focusing, opening, and child-process self-exit do not run `on_hide`.

Example intended shape:

```kdl
popup_defaults {
    on_close {
        command "yzn-sidebar-refresh"
    }
    on_hide {
        command "yzn-sidebar-refresh"
    }
}

popups {
    git {
        command "lazygit"
        toggle_close_behavior "hide"
    }
}
```

## Scope

In scope:
- Add an optional `on_hide` command hook to configured popup specs and raw transient popup specs.
- Parse `on_hide` with the same argv/cwd shape and validation rules as `on_close`.
- Add optional `on_close` and `on_hide` hook defaults under `popup_defaults`.
- Let per-popup `on_close` and `on_hide` override the corresponding default hook.
- Run `on_hide` after `hide_pane_with_id` for focused-popup toggle hide and displaced keep-alive hide.
- Preserve `on_close` semantics exactly for close-mode popups and explicit close actions.
- Update README docs and focused contract/unit tests for hook defaults, per-popup override, invalid hook rejection, and hide/displacement behavior.

Out of scope:
- Do not add Yazelix, Yazi, git, sidebar, `yzn`, or `yzx` knowledge to `yzpp`.
- Do not add polling, timers, file watchers, or git status logic.
- Do not run `on_hide` for open/focus/show events.
- Do not run `on_hide` when a child process exits by itself unless a future Zellij event makes that a separate explicit contract.
- Do not add a broad lifecycle event system beyond `on_hide` plus existing `on_close`.
- Do not add an opt-out/disable syntax for inherited hooks unless implementation finds it necessary; keep the first slice to explicit defaults plus per-popup override.

## Implementation Notes

1. Extend the spec model with `on_hide: Option<TransientPopupCommandHook>` beside `on_close`.
2. Extend `PopupSpecDefaults` to carry optional default `on_close` and `on_hide` hooks.
3. Reuse the existing hook parser and launch-plan logic for both per-popup hooks and default hooks; avoid a second hook implementation.
4. In `build_configured_spec`, inherit each default hook only when the popup does not define that hook explicitly.
5. Thread `on_hide` through any transient pane candidate views that need it for displacement.
6. In the toggle-focused hide path, run the hook after hiding the pane and before or after responding `hidden`, whichever keeps behavior easiest to test without changing CLI responses.
7. In displacement, run `on_hide` only for candidates with `toggle_close_behavior Hide`.
8. Keep hook execution best-effort and consistent with current `on_close`: failures should not crash the plugin unless current hook behavior already does.

## Verification

- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` should exit 0.
- `cargo test --lib` should exit 0 with coverage for `on_hide` parsing, invalid hook rejection, inherited hook defaults, per-popup hook override, focused hide hook planning, and displaced hidden popup hook planning.
- `cargo build --target wasm32-wasip1 --profile release` should exit 0.
- `nix build .#yazelix_zellij_popup --no-link` should exit 0.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if Zellij's hide API cannot provide a reliable point to run a hook after the pane becomes hidden.
- Stop if the implementation requires Yazelix-specific assumptions or side effects in this generic plugin.
- Stop if hook ordering would make CLI pipe responses observably incompatible with existing `hidden` behavior.
- Stop if hook defaults require a broad inheritance/disable system; keep only explicit defaults plus per-popup override or split the default behavior into a follow-up.

## Maintenance Notes

Dependency decision: no new Rust crates expected; this should reuse existing hook parsing and std-owned command execution. Rejected alternative: `on_dismiss`, because `on_hide` is easier to understand when paired with existing `on_close`: close kills the pane, hide preserves it. Accepted edge-trim: `yzpp` owning generic hook defaults should delete repeated generated hook glue from YZN without making `yzpp` know about YZN.
close_reason: Implemented generic on_hide and popup_defaults lifecycle hooks in yzpp. Added per-popup on_hide parsing for configured and raw specs, inherited on_close/on_hide defaults with per-popup override, and runtime hook execution after hide-mode focused toggles and hide-mode displacement. Updated README with generic hook examples. Verification: cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo test --lib; cargo build --target wasm32-wasip1 --profile release; nix build .#yazelix_zellij_popup --no-link.
